data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0112-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — run 0010, opening Ground (session 0109-ground)

- Useful context (live Quot cone, `AJC.picrep`): `Grassmannian.representable` is closed axiom-clean (`Picard/GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean` + `GrassmannianRepresentability.lean`, both `sorry`-free). The remaining Quot leaves live in `Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` — **5 `sorry`s** at lines 380 `pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite` (index bookkeeping), 419 `CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback`, 432 `HasProperSupport.of_isPullback`, 457 `hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap` (H⁰ flat base change), 844 `QuotScheme`.

- Consistency note: `thm:quot_representable` (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:4374`) is now `\notready` (was falsely `\leanok`). Its Lean target `QuotScheme` is unproved and **false as pinned** — proper+lofinite-type over arbitrary quasi-coherent `L,E`, weaker than the correct projective+very-ample+coherent statement the blueprint holds (`I-0118`; Mathlib v4.31 lacks projectivity vocabulary, so the signature can't yet be made faithful).

- Related bricks: `CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` needs the same stalk-of-pullback infrastructure as the FBC leaf `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (`CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`; route analysis in memory `I-0076`/`I-0083`). Functor-law and ModuleCat-hom recipes for this chapter are in memory `I-0113`/`I-0088`; hilbertPolynomial substrate in `I-0109`, 01XJ pushforward-qcoh in `I-0120`.

- Other active cones if widening scope: `AJC.fbc` (3 leaves in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`), `AJC.pic0av` tangent-space `tangentSpaceIso` (task T10, `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`; attack path in `I-0061`/`I-0082`), and `ALB.codim1` gated on the single Milne 3.3 leaf `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`; chain notes in `I-0065`).

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

Now that most of the necessary architecture is already made, `GrassmannianQuot`, `FlatteningStratification`, `GenericFlatnessGeometric`, etc, you should already commit to the hard sorries and close the representability task.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).
- Consumers: quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` (synthesized from `[HasRationalPoint C]`) — `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` in `IdentityComponent.lean` now carry it. `Pic0Scheme C := GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` is now a real def (axiom-clean), gated on `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.
- **Still blocked, do not re-probe**: `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` QC∧GeomIrred conjunct (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1 translate-cover argument, not in Mathlib v4.31); `degree`/`kPoints_iff_kerDegree` (Quot/Hilbert χ endgame); `finrank_eq_genus`, `Pic0.smooth`, `Pic0.proper` (need tangentSpaceIso resp. Chevalley–Rosenlicht).
- **tangentSpaceIso attack path opened**: the ring-level truncated-exponential splitting is DONE (`Picard/DualNumberUnits.lean`, axiom-clean: `(R[ε])ˣ ≃* Rˣ × (R,+)` + naturality via `mapRingHom`; blueprint `sec:pic0_trunc_exp`, 15 nodes). Next bricks in order: (1) dual-number points of `Pic0Scheme` ↔ `ker(picSharp(Spec k[ε]) → picSharp(Spec k))` via `(representable C).homEquiv` (clopen ⟹ Pic vs Pic⁰ dual-number points at 0 agree); (2) sheaf-level sequence on `C_ε = C ×ₖ Spec k[ε]` (identify `O_{C_ε}` sections with `O_C(U)[ε]`); (3) Čech `H¹(O^×)` cocycle classification of line bundles trivial mod ε.
- **FBC naturality leaves: precise missing layer (post-heart-closure, run 0009 T2; supersedes '4 leaf sorries' notes).** FBC has exactly **3 sorries**, all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`: the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` ~1690, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` ~1758) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (197). `FlatBaseChange.lean` is sorry-free (mate stubs deleted).

- **The naturality fields are NOT quick wins.** Both components are ≪≫-chains through `pushPull_sigma_iso` + `Pi.mapIso` of per-σ isos. Missing layer, in order: (1) a transport formula expressing `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).map φ` through `pushPull_sigma_iso` as a matrix of restrictions `pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ) ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_τ)` (only `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`/`_π_incl` exist, in `CechSectionIdentificationBase/Leg`); (2) per-σ restriction compatibility of the heavy per-σ isos (`pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`) — coherence through the `AffinePushPullEssImage` essImage/adjoint constructions. Estimate: multi-hundred LOC each; one focused session per leaf at best.
- **Heart-closure recipe that landed** (session 0011 surgery, verified 0013): paste `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` onto the ambient square (`IsPullback.paste_vert`), convert with the converse `isPushout_of_isPullback_SpecMap` (`IsPullback.of_map_of_faithful` on `Scheme.Spec`, then `.unop.flip`), identify the pullback corner with `coverInterOpen 𝒰' σ` via the slice iso, rewrite corner sections (`coverInterOpen_baseChange_restrictedMap_comm` + `pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde` + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`), finish with `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`. `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000` scoped per declaration suffices.
- Recipe: building ModuleCat-R morphisms into sheaf-of-modules section objects (v4.31) — the ConcreteCategory.ofHom + presheaf-obj pattern, and the let-fvar IsIso gotcha

Landed in `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean`, T12 run 0010). When you need `f : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ <sections object>` (e.g. to feed `tilde.map`):

- **Target the honest `ModuleCat R` object** `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)` — this is literally the domain module of Mathlib's `fromTildeΓ`/`toOpen`. Do NOT target `moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` via `ModuleCat.ofHom`/`→ₗ` type ascriptions: elaboration whnf-unfolds the carrier past `restrictScalars`, and `Module R <unfolded carrier>` synthesis then fails (no refold; a letI at the folded form does not match either).
- **Construct via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := ..., map_add' := ..., map_smul' := ... }`** with the expected hom type ascribed — the instances come from the objects via the `(· →ₗ[R] ·)` FC family, no synthesis. A bare `let blin : M →ₗ[R] <object> := ...` fails; skipping the standalone LinearMap and going straight to the categorical hom works.
- **map_smul' across compHom-vs-restrictScalars actions**: `change` the goal to the native-action form `(ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom r • f x`, then `rw [← hact]; exact h1` where `hact : appLE V ⊤ e = (ΓSpecIso R).inv` (proved by `rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 ⊤), op_id, map_id, comp_id]`).
- **IsIso of `tilde.map b'` for a `let`-bound b'**: `haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by rw [show tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' from rfl]; infer_instance` FAILS (zeta-expansion mismatch between goal and local instance). Use `inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((tilde.functor _).map b'))` — works even without `clear_value`.
- **Unit-component bijectivity at open immersions**: `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app (restrictAdjunction j) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction j) N` + `restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app` (plain presheaf restriction along `j''ᵁj⁻¹ᵁV = V`) + component of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. When `rw [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply]` fails with "target not type-correct under instances transparency" (`Γ((𝟭 X.Modules).obj N, V)` forms), do `change`-restatement at the type-correct form, then close with `exact congrArg (fun φ => (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom φ) y) hcomp` where `hcomp : f ≫ g = 𝟙 _` — both sides defeq, no rewriting in the wall.
- **Σ-pair trace**: `tilde.toOpen_map_app` (naturality) + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` at `⊤` (+ `homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by `Subsingleton.elim`) give `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ).app ⊤ ∘ toOpen = b'` elementwise, via `congrArg`-chains only.
- **Recipes: module-level dévissage in Lean v4.31 (landed in FlatteningStratification GenericFreeness, run 0010 T12 r2) — reuse for any prime-filtration/SES argument.**

- **ModuleCat SES arguments need ONE universe**: `ModuleCat.ofHom {X Y : Type v}` forces all modules of a `ShortComplex (ModuleCat R)` into the same universe. Binders `{M₁ M₂ M₃ : Type*}` give distinct universes and produce baffling failures — instance goals displayed as raw `Quot ⇑(OreLocalization.oreEqv ...)` (LocalizedModule unfolded past its instance heads) plus universe-mismatched `Eq.{max u_1 u_4}` vs `Eq.{max u_1 u_2}`. Pin `Type v`. The dévissage eliminator is single-universe anyway.
- **`set S := Submonoid.powers (f*g)` breaks instance unification** in localized-module types (same family as the known let-fvar IsIso gotcha): instance search will not zeta-unfold the set-fvar when unifying `Localization S'` heads. Write the submonoid term explicitly everywhere; `let`-bound MAPS (data) are fine — only types/instances mind.
- **Quantified-instance motive** for `IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime` when the property lives over a DIFFERENT ring A than the induction ring B: `motive N _ iMB _ := ∀ (iA : Module A N), @IsScalarTower A B N Algebra.toSMul iMB.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul iA.toDistribMulAction.toMulAction.toSMul → @P A N _ _ iA`. Cases: subsingleton via @-application; quotient case transports along `(e.restrictScalars A).symm` (canonical tower on B⧸p is instance-found); exact case builds `letI iA₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)` + tower `⟨fun a b n => by change (a • b) • n = algebraMap A B a • b • n; rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]⟩`; B-linear maps restrict via `LinearMap.restrictScalars A` and inj/surj/Exact transfer definitionally. Final application `... ‹Module A M› ‹IsScalarTower A B M›` unifies. Compiled essentially first-try.
- **Two-localization freeness transport** (`M_f` free ⟹ `M_{fg}` free) WITHOUT IsLocalizedModule-composition: `(IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayRight f g).toAlgebra` + `IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (awayToAwayRight_eq ..).symm`, then equiv-chain `congr(refl, isBaseChange f) .symm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ isBaseChange (f*g)` and `Module.Free.tensor` + `Module.Free.of_equiv'`. Give every LinearEquiv a fully-typed `have` — `refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ e` with open source type sticks on RingHomCompTriple metavariables.
- **Localized SES**: `IsLocalizedModule.map/map_injective/map_surjective/map_exact` at `mkLinearMap`s, upgrade R-linear→Loc-linear with `LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization` (coe unchanged, so Function.Exact/Injective/Surjective transfer by defeq); `ShortComplex.mk (ofHom u') (ofHom v') (ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun x => hexact.apply_apply_eq_zero x))`; ShortExact via `moduleCat_exact_iff` + `mono_iff_injective` + `epi_iff_surjective`; finish `ModuleCat.free_shortExact`.
- **refine-under-binder**: `refine subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 fun x => ?_` fails (?_ under lambda); use `apply` + `intro` + `induction x using LocalizedModule.induction_on with | _ m s => ...`. `Submonoid.mk_smul` bridges `(⟨c,hc⟩ : ↥S) • m` to `c • m`.
- **T13 genus-0 / Route-C retirement: `WeilDivisor.lean` is DUAL-PURPOSE — carve, do not delete.**

The genus split is *already* mostly retired: `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, and `genusZeroWitness`/`positiveGenusWitness` are deleted; `Jacobian C` is uniform via `picardJacobianWitness`. The remaining T13 work is residue cleanup, with one dependency trap.

**Trap — `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` serves two masters.**

- Its `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` type and `Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation are the codim-1 substrate that `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`ALB.codim1`, live north-star path) imports and uses — the `coheight = 1` convention (`CodimOneExtension.lean:177`) and the order-valuation appeal (`:1823`).
- The `degree` / `degree_hom` / `principal_degree_zero` / `positivePart` / RR→ℙ¹ block (5 sorries, `-- Route C PAUSE`) exists only to prove the now-obsolete `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`. That block is the Route-C / genus-0 dead weight.

**Consequence:** a wholesale delete of `WeilDivisor.lean` breaks the codim-1 extension build. The dependency-safe shape is a surgical carve of the RR/degree/ℙ¹ portion plus the file header (which frames the whole file around `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`), retaining the `PrimeDivisor`/`order` substrate.

**Also live, not genus-0:** `RigidityLemma.lean` and blueprint `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` are the sorry-free rigidity-lemma + Milne §I.1–I.3 corollaries used by the uniform Albanese construction; only their "feed the genus-0 base case (Route C)" prose is stale — clean the prose, keep the math.
- **T12 r-current: hilbertPolynomial headline def CLOSED (real definition) — updates the 'QuotScheme 8 sorries' triage of I-0089.**

- New file `Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean` (~230 LOC, axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, LSP + full `lake build` green, 8647 jobs): `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` is now REAL — the unique `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]` eventually matching the graded Hilbert function `m ↦ dim_{κ(s)} Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`), junk `0` outside the regime. Fibre = Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.fiber`; restrictions via `Scheme.Modules.pullback (π.fiberι s)` (`Hom.fiberModule`); twists via `SectionGradedRing.moduleTensorPow`; κ(s)-structure = `Module.compHom` along `Hom.fiberResidueMap` (`ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ (fiberToSpecResidueField).appTop`) — all compiled essentially first-try, no defeq walls.
- API: `hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` (unconditional uniqueness via `Polynomial.eq_of_infinite_eval_eq`), `existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb` + `hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly` (existence from `IsRatHilb`, i.e. from the sorry-free `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` engine via Mathlib `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`/`coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`).
- **QuotScheme.lean now 7 sorries** (4 headline: QuotFunctor/Grassmannian/Grassmannian.representable/QuotScheme + 3 FBC-gated base-change leaves). QuotScheme.lean now imports HilbertPolynomial (which imports SectionGradedRing + GradedHilbertSerre; both were previously leaf files — no cycle, verified).
- **Remaining Hilbert lane, in order**: (1) `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` — Serre finite-generation of `⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})` over `⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^{⊗m})` for proper X_s + ample L_s, THE deep remaining input (needs ampleness vocabulary; multi-session); (2) the DirectSum-decomposition bridge instantiating `gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` at the fibre section module (external ⊕ → internal ℳ decomposition + degree-1 commuting endos from a κ(s)-basis of R₁ + Module.Finite over MvPolynomial); (3) then `QuotFunctor` def can consume `hilbertPolynomial` (its statement needs no new theory — flatness/coherence predicates + Setoid quotient + pullback functoriality, one focused session).
- T12: QuotFunctor + Grassmannian are REAL functors (QuotFunctorDef.lean); coherence recipe + remaining leaves

**The two headline Quot-chapter definitions are real** (run 0010 T12, `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean`): `Scheme.QuotFunctor` = Setoid quotient of `QuotFamily` (finitely presented + `CoherentSheafFlat` + `HasProperSupport` + eventual-match Hilbert condition via `hilbertFunction`), morphism action = pullback along `quotBaseMap` ($\mathrm{id}_X \times_S \psi$); `Scheme.Grassmannian` = rank-$d$ locally free quotients of $V_T$ (deliberately NOT the Quot special case — the locally-free form is the base-independent universal property matching the merged absolute `Grassmannian.functor`). Both `(Over S)^{op} \to \mathrm{Type}\,(u+1)` (forced universe bump, picSharp precedent). Old stubs deleted from `QuotScheme.lean` (now 3 sorries = the FBC leaves).

- **Functor laws** proved from Mathlib pseudofunctor coherences via two app-level lemmas `Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence` / `pullback_comp_app_coherence(_inv)`: prove by `subst` (state triangle equalities with free RHS variables!), one simp pass on the NatTrans law, congrArg-composition with `Iso.hom_inv_id_app_assoc`, then close by `exact lawV` — **comp-with-identity padding is DEFEQ in `X.Modules`; do not fight the instance-transparency walls with simp**.
- Remaining 6 sorries in the file, all TRUE: `pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite` (pure index bookkeeping; whnf through the 8-layer presentation transport exceeds 8M heartbeats — needs a new idea, do not retry inferInstanceAs-defeq blindly), `CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` (stalk route; needs the same stalk-of-pullback brick as FBC pullback_preservesFiniteLimits), `HasProperSupport.of_isPullback` (annihilator pullback inclusion + properness base change), `hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap` ($H^0$ flat base change along residue extensions on the proper support), and the two deep endgames `Grassmannian.representable`, `QuotScheme`.
- Blueprint: new subsection `sec:quot_base_change` with 4 lemma nodes (complete proofs); `def:quot_functor`/`def:grassmannian_scheme` now honestly `\leanok`; `def:grassmannian_scheme` no longer `\uses` `def:quot_functor`.
- **Stacks 01XJ pushforward-qcoh CLOSED, axiom-clean (run 0010 T12) — the qcqs section-localization engine is now reusable substrate.**

Ground-verified: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (`Picard/QuotScheme.lean:3950`, delegated at `:4170`) is a **real theorem**, axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (no `sorryAx`). QuotScheme real-sorry count dropped (only the FBC-gated base-change leaves at `:5079/:5130/:5180` remain as bare tactic `sorry`).

- **Engine (Stacks 01P0 beyond affine):** `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isCompact` (`:3441`) — for quasi-coherent `M` and a **qcqs** open `W`, `Γ(M,W) → Γ(M,D(g))` is a localization. Proved by Mayer–Vietoris on compact opens (`compact_open_induction_on`), torsion + surjectivity halves as separate public lemmas; affine base case = the existing gap2 keystone.
- **Converse transport:** `isIso_fromTildeΓ_pullback_fromSpec_of_isLocalizedModule` (`:3585`) — per-basic-open section localization at an affine open forces `IsIso (fromTildeΓ)` of the `fromSpec`-pullback (the gap2 chain run backwards). General "sections localize ⟹ quasi-coherent" mechanism the project lacked; also the right substrate for future 01P0-type needs (Route-C coherent-χ, qcqs cohomology).
- **v4.31 recipes:** MV-induction over finite-cover descent; eqToHom-free opens transport via mutual restriction; `set`-fvar rw leakage → `clear_value`; `@asIso`-explicit-instance fix for the `Presentation.ofIsIso` category-instance diamond.
- **Blueprint:** `lem:pushforward_isQuasicoherent` (`Picard_QuotScheme.tex:5412`) now carries the REAL proof (`\uses{lem:pushforward_section_localization, lem:fromTildeGamma_pullback_fromSpec_converse}`); the old "right adjoints preserve quasi-coherence" sketch is explicitly disclaimed (adjointness ⇒ colimit preservation only). Previously-dangling `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen` now has its `\label` (`:5255`). Related: [[I-0075]] (pullback-qcoh 01BG), [[I-0113]], [[I-0109]].

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

300	/-- The comparison isomorphism `b^* g^* V ≅ (gb)^* V` attached to a commuting
301	triangle `b ≫ g = gb` of schemes: the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
302	`pullbackComp` followed by transport `pullbackCongr` along the triangle.
303	Instantiated with `Over.w ψ` it matches the test modules `V_T` of the
304	Grassmannian functor, and with `quotBaseMap_fst` the pulled-back sheaves
305	`E_T`, `L_T` of the Quot functor. -/
306	noncomputable def pullbackTriangleIso {Z Y W : Scheme.{u}} {b : Z ⟶ Y} {g : Y ⟶ W}
307	    {gb : Z ⟶ W} (h : b ≫ g = gb) (V : W.Modules) :
308	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj V) ≅
309	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gb).obj V :=
310	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b g).app V ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app V

312	/-! ## §2. Base-change preservation of the Quot-family conditions
313	
314	Nitsure §1: "as properness and flatness are preserved by base-change, and as
315	tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of `⟨F, q⟩` … is well-defined."
316	The four preservation facts, in the forms consumed by the pullback action of
317	the Quot functor.  The first is proved (right-exactness of the pullback and
318	transport of finite presentations along the per-slice bridge); the remaining
319	three are recorded as named typed `sorry` leaves with honest statements —
320	see the blueprint nodes `lem:coherent_flat_base_change`,
321	`lem:proper_support_base_change`, `lem:hilbert_fibre_base_change`. -/
322	
323	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

326	/-- Term-mode variant of `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`
327	(`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`) — the same per-slice transport of a
328	presentation of `F.over A` to a presentation of `(g^* F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)`,
329	restated without tactic-`set` wrappers so that the finiteness of the
330	presentation can be read off the transport chain by instance search
331	(every step is `Presentation.map` or `Presentation.ofIsIso`). -/
332	noncomputable def Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
333	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) :
334	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)).Presentation :=
335	  letI P1 : (F.restrict A.ι).Presentation := presentationRestrictOfOver A F A P le_rfl
336	  letI gA : (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme ⟶ A.toScheme := g.resLE A (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) le_rfl
337	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
338	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) := inferInstance
339	  letI P2 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι)).Presentation :=
340	    @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P1
341	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA) hpc (pullbackUnitIso gA).symm
342	  letI e : (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).obj (F.restrict A.ι) ≅
343	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι :=
344	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback gA).mapIso
345	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback A.ι).app F) ≪≫
346	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp gA A.ι).app F ≪≫
347	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)).app F ≪≫
348	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι g).app F).symm ≪≫
349	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).app
350	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm
351	  letI P5 : (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).restrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).ι).Presentation :=
352	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} e.hom P2
353	  letI eV := modulesOverOpensEquivalence (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
354	  letI P6 : (eV.inverse.obj (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).Presentation :=
355	    SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
356	      (overOpensIsoRestrict (g ⁻¹ᵁ A) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F)).symm.hom P5
357	  letI ηV : eV.functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (g ⁻¹ᵁ A).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
358	      SheafOfModules.unit (Sheaf.over Y.ringCatSheaf (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)) :=
359	    overOpensFunctorUnitIso (X := Y) (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)
360	  letI P7 : (eV.functor.obj (eV.inverse.obj
361	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A)))).Presentation :=
362	    P6.map eV.functor ηV.symm
363	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u}
364	    (eV.counitIso.app (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ A))).hom P7

366	/-- The per-slice pullback presentation transport preserves finiteness: every
367	step of `Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation` (and of the underlying
368	`presentationRestrictOfOver` / `presentationOverOpens` transports) is a
369	`Presentation.map` or a `Presentation.ofIsIso`, both of which preserve the
370	generator and relation index types (definitionally — `Presentation.map` via
371	`presentationOfIsCokernelFree` keeps `generators.I`/`relations.I`, and
372	`Presentation.ofIsIso` transports along `GeneratingSections.ofEpi`, which
373	keeps the index).  The `whnf`-reduction certifying this definitional identity
374	exceeds practical heartbeat budgets through the eight-layer transport chain,
375	so the finiteness transfer is recorded as a typed `sorry` (a mechanical
376	index-bookkeeping fact, no mathematical content). -/
377	lemma Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
378	    (F : X.Modules) (A : X.Opens) (P : (F.over A).Presentation) [P.IsFinite] :
379	    (Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F A P).IsFinite := by
380	  sorry

382	/-- **Pullback preserves finite presentation** (Stacks 01BK-adjacent; the
383	finite-presentation refinement of `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`).  The
384	per-slice transport of `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` preserves the
385	index types of the presentations, so the pullback of a finitely presented
386	sheaf of modules along an arbitrary scheme morphism is finitely presented. -/
387	theorem Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
388	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
389	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsFinitePresentation := by
390	  obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ := hF.exists_quasicoherentData
391	  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
392	    intro W x hx
393	    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
394	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
395	    exact ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
396	      ⟨hx, (leOfHom gi) hU'⟩⟩
397	  let q' : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).QuasicoherentData :=
398	    { I := q.I
399	      X := fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i
400	      coversTop := hcov
401	      presentation := fun i => Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation g F (q.X i) (q.presentation i) }
402	  have hfin : q'.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by
403	    apply SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation.mk
404	    intro i
405	    exact Modules.pullbackSlicePresentation_isFinite g F _ _
406	  exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q'.shrink, hfin⟩ }

408	/-- **Flatness over the base is stable under base change** (Stacks 01U9,
409	lifted to the coherent-sheaf flatness predicate `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`
410	along a cartesian square).  Affine-locally this is `Module.Flat.baseChange`
411	threaded through the quasi-coherent section calculus: the stalk of `g'^* F`
412	is the base change of the stalk of `F`, and the affine-pair predicate is
413	stalk-local for quasi-coherent modules (Stacks 00HT). -/
414	theorem CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback
415	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
416	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hqc : F.IsQuasicoherent)
417	    (hF : CoherentSheafFlat f F) :
418	    CoherentSheafFlat f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
419	  sorry

421	/-- **Proper support is stable under base change** (Nitsure §1; Stacks 01W4 +
422	056H).  For a finitely presented `F`, the annihilator of `g'^* F` contains
423	the pullback of the annihilator of `F`, so the schematic support of `g'^* F`
424	factors through the base change of the schematic support of `F` by a closed
425	immersion; properness is stable under base change and under precomposition
426	with closed immersions. -/
427	theorem Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback
428	    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
429	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hfp : F.IsFinitePresentation)
430	    (hF : Modules.HasProperSupport f F) :
431	    Modules.HasProperSupport f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
432	  sorry
433	
434	/-- **The fibrewise graded Hilbert function is invariant under base change**
435	(Nitsure §1, the implicit invariance behind the decomposition
436	`Quot = ∐_Φ Quot^Φ`).  For `t' : T'` over `t := ψ(t')`, the fibre of
437	`X_{T'} → T'` at `t'` is the base change of the fibre of `X_T → T` at `t`
438	along the residue extension `κ(t) → κ(t')` (`quotBaseSquare` pasting), and
439	`H⁰` of the twists of a finitely presented module with proper support
440	commutes with base field extension (flat base change over a field, Stacks
441	02KH), so the `κ`-dimensions agree.  Quasi-coherence of the twisting module
442	`L` is required for the flat-base-change step (for an arbitrary sheaf of
443	modules the statement can fail), matching the line-bundle `L` of
444	`def:quot_functor`. -/
445	theorem hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules)
446	    [L.IsQuasicoherent]
447	    {T T' : Over S} (ψ : T' ⟶ T) (F : (Limits.pullback π T.hom).Modules)
448	    (hfp : F.IsFinitePresentation)
449	    (hps : Modules.HasProperSupport (pullback.snd π T.hom) F)
450	    (t' : (T'.left : Scheme.{u})) (m : ℕ) :
451	    hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T'.hom)
452	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T'.hom)).obj L)
453	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (quotBaseMap π ψ)).obj F) t' m
454	      = hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T.hom)
455	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj L) F
456	        (ψ.left.base t') m := by
457	  sorry

459	/-! ## §3. Families of quotients and the Quot functor -/
460	
461	section QuotFunctor
462	
463	variable [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
464	
465	/-- A **family of quotients of `E` parametrised by `T`** with Hilbert
466	polynomial `Φ` (Nitsure §1; the unbundled datum whose equivalence classes
467	form the value of the Quot functor `def:quot_functor` at `T`):
468	
469	* a finitely presented sheaf of modules `F` on the relative product
470	  `X_T = X ×_S T` (the coherence encoding of the flattening-stratification
471	  input; over the locally noetherian regime finite presentation ⟺
472	  coherence), flat over `T` and with schematic support proper over `T`;
473	* an epimorphism `q : E_T ⟶ F` from the pullback of `E` along the first
474	  projection;
475	* at every point `t : T`, the graded Hilbert function
476	  `m ↦ dim_{κ(t)} Γ((X_T)_t, F_t ⊗ L_t^{⊗m})` eventually agrees with `Φ` —
477	  by `Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` this says exactly that the
478	  Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` relative to `L|_{X_t}`
479	  (`def:hilbert_polynomial`) *is* `Φ`, and it excludes the junk-value
480	  coincidence when no polynomial matches. -/
481	structure QuotFamily (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] (L E : X.Modules)
482	    (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) (T : Over S) : Type (u + 1) where
483	  /-- The quotient sheaf on the relative product `X ×_S T`. -/
484	  F : (Limits.pullback π T.hom).Modules
485	  /-- `F` is finitely presented (coherent, in the locally noetherian regime). -/
486	  isFinitePresentation : F.IsFinitePresentation
487	  /-- `F` is flat over `T`. -/
488	  flat : CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π T.hom) F
489	  /-- The schematic support of `F` is proper over `T`. -/
490	  properSupport : Modules.HasProperSupport (pullback.snd π T.hom) F
491	  /-- The quotient map from the pullback of `E`. -/
492	  q : (Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj E ⟶ F
493	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism. -/
494	  epi : Epi q
495	  /-- At every `t : T`, the graded Hilbert function of the fibre of `F`
496	  twisted by `L` eventually agrees with `Φ`. -/
497	  hilb : ∀ t : (T.left : Scheme.{u}), ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ m : ℕ, N < m →
498	    Φ.eval (m : ℚ) = (hilbertFunction (pullback.snd π T.hom)
499	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π T.hom)).obj L) F t m : ℚ)

750	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
751	rank-`d` locally free quotients of a module `V` on a noetherian base `S`
752	(`def:grassmannian_scheme`, [Nitsure] §1, Exercise (2)): an `S`-scheme
753	`T → S` is sent to the set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of rank-`d`
754	locally free quotients `q : V_T ↠ F` (`Scheme.LocallyFreeQuotient`), and a
755	morphism to the pullback of quotients.  In the locally noetherian regime
756	this agrees with the Quot-functor special case
757	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` of the blueprint (a flat finitely
758	presented module with constant fibre dimension `d` is locally free of rank
759	`d`); the locally-free encoding is the form valid over an arbitrary base and
760	the one matched by the merged chart construction
761	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`). -/
762	noncomputable def Grassmannian (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
763	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u + 1) where
764	  obj T := Quotient (LocallyFreeQuotient.setoid V d T.unop)
765	  map {T T'} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong g.unop)
766	    (fun _ _ h => LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong_rel g.unop h))
767	  map_id T := by
768	    ext z
769	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
770	    | _ x =>
771	      change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong (𝟙 T.unop) x)
772	        = Quotient.mk _ x
773	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
774	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app x.F, ?_⟩
775	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V).inv ≫
776	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Over.Hom.left (𝟙 T.unop))).map x.q) ≫
777	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
778	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.app x.F
779	        = x.q
780	      rw [Category.assoc,
781	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.id_left T.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
782	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).hom.naturality x.q]
783	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence (Over.id_left T.unop)
784	        (Over.w (𝟙 T.unop)) V
785	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc]
786	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
787	      rfl
788	  map_comp {T T' T''} g h := by
789	    ext z
790	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
791	    | _ x =>
792	      change Quotient.mk _ (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong (h.unop ≫ g.unop) x)
793	        = Quotient.mk _
794	          (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong h.unop (LocallyFreeQuotient.pullbackAlong g.unop x))
795	      refine Quotient.sound
796	        ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).app x.F ≪≫
797	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
798	      change ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w (h.unop ≫ g.unop)) V).inv ≫
799	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Over.Hom.left (h.unop ≫ g.unop))).map x.q) ≫
800	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).hom.app x.F ≫
801	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).inv.app x.F
802	        = (pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w h.unop) V).inv ≫
803	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback h.unop.left).map
804	            ((pullbackTriangleIso (Over.w g.unop) V).inv ≫
805	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop.left).map x.q)
806	      rw [Category.assoc,
807	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop)).hom.naturality_assoc x.q,
808	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h.unop.left g.unop.left).inv.naturality x.q]
809	      have key := Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence_inv
810	        h.unop.left g.unop.left (Over.comp_left _ _ _ h.unop g.unop) T.unop.hom
811	        (Over.w g.unop) (Over.w (h.unop ≫ g.unop)) (Over.w h.unop) V
812	      simp only [pullbackTriangleIso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.app_inv, Category.assoc,
813	        Functor.map_comp]
814	      rw [reassoc_of% key]
815	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
816	      rfl
817	
818	/- **Representability of the Grassmannian** (`thm:grassmannian_representable`)
819	now lives in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianRepresentability.lean`
820	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable`), stated with the
821	blueprint hypotheses (`V` locally free of rank `r`, `1 ≤ d ≤ r`) that the
822	earlier skeleton here omitted — without them the bare statement quantifies
823	over arbitrary sheaves of modules `V`, which is not the [Nitsure] §1 theorem
824	and is not known to be true.  The proof consumes the merged absolute chart
825	construction (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents`,
826	`GrassmannianQuot.lean`), which this file deliberately does not import. -/
827	
828	end Grassmannian
829	
830	/-! ## §5. Representability of the Quot scheme -/
831	
832	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman;
833	`thm:quot_representable`, [Nitsure] §5): for a noetherian `S`, a projective
834	`π` (encoded: proper and locally of finite type), a line bundle `L`, a
835	coherent `E` and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor is representable by an
836	`S`-scheme.  Proof route (Nitsure §5): boundedness by Castelnuovo–Mumford
837	regularity, embedding into a Grassmannian by pushing forward twists,
838	flattening stratification (`AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification`),
839	and the valuative criterion for the closed embedding. -/
840	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
841	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
842	    (L E : X.Modules) [L.IsQuasicoherent] (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
843	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
844	  sorry
845	
846	end Scheme
847	
848	end AlgebraicGeometry

101	open CategoryTheory Limits
102	
103	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
104	
105	namespace Scheme
106	
107	/-- **`C` has a `k`-rational point**: the structural morphism `C.hom` admits a
108	section `σ : Spec k ⟶ C.left`. For the smooth proper geometrically integral
109	curves of this project this is the pointing `P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec k)) ⟶ C`
110	already threaded through the Albanese statements (`AlgebraicJacobian.IsAlbanese`).
111	
112	This is the hypothesis of Kleiman §2 **Thm 2.5**: together with
113	`O_S = f_* O_X` universally (automatic for proper geometrically integral
114	fibers), a section forces the comparison maps
115	`Pic_{X/S}(T) → Pic_{(X/S)ét}(T) → Pic_{(X/S)fppf}(T)` to be bijective, so
116	the *plain* relative Picard functor is already the étale sheaf that FGA
117	representability (Kleiman §4) represents. -/
118	class HasRationalPoint {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Prop where
119	  nonempty_section :
120	    Nonempty {σ : Spec (.of k) ⟶ C.left // σ ≫ C.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))}
121	
122	namespace PicScheme
123	
124	/-! ## §0. The relative Picard functor and the divisor functor
125	
126	`picSharp` is the honest relative Picard functor of sibling
127	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (Kleiman §2 Def `df:Pfs`), with the group
128	structure forgotten (representability is a statement about the underlying
129	set-valued functor; the group structure is transported back onto the
130	representing scheme by `groupSchemeStructure` below).
131	
132	`divFunctor` (the relative-effective-divisor functor `Div_{C/k}`, Kleiman §3
133	Thm `th:repDiv`) is still a `⟨sorry⟩`-backed carrier awaiting the Quot-scheme
134	engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`). -/
135	
136	/-- **The relative Picard functor** `Pic^♯_{C/k} : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type (u+1)`,
137	`T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` — the set-valued shadow of the group-valued
138	presheaf `PicSharp.relPresheaf` of `Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (the honest
139	`H_T`-coset carrier of Kleiman §2 Def `df:Pfs`).
140	
141	Run-0008 rewire: this replaces the former opaque
142	`Classical.choice`-of-`⟨sorry⟩` placeholder; it is now a REAL definition with
143	computational content, so `RepresentableBy` witnesses against it carry a
144	genuine natural bijection `(T ⟶ Pic_{C/k}) ≃ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)`.
145	
146	Universe note: the values live in `Type (u+1)` because the line-bundle
147	carrier `LineBundle.OnProduct` is a quotient of (large) sheaves of modules.
148	`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic, so representability by a
149	(`Type u`-homed) scheme remains a well-formed — and, under a rational point,
150	true — statement. -/
151	noncomputable def picSharp {k : Type u} [Field k]
152	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
153	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] :
154	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1) :=
155	  PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}
156	
157	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the relative-divisor functor `Div_{C/k}`.
158	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
159	this carrier. (The `Nonempty` statement is trivially true — any functor
160	witnesses it — so this carrier pattern is sound; the real content arrives
161	when `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` lands and the carrier becomes a re-export.) -/
162	class HasDivFunctor {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : Prop where
163	  has_div_functor : Nonempty ((Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1))
164	
165	/-- **File-internal placeholder** for the relative-divisor functor
166	`Div_{C/k} : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type (u+1)`, sending a `k`-scheme `T` to the set of
167	relative effective Cartier divisors on `C ×_k T` flat over `T`.

169	The blueprint chapter `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` pins the Quot/Hilbert engine

172	Thm. `th:repDiv`). Once `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` lands the present def
173	collapses to a one-line re-export. Until then it is extracted from
174	`HasDivFunctor.has_div_functor` via `Classical.choice` — an OPAQUE functor
175	with no defining property; nothing downstream may rely on its values. -/
176	noncomputable def divFunctor {k : Type u} [Field k]
177	    (_C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [HasDivFunctor _C] :
178	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1) :=
179	  Classical.choice (HasDivFunctor.has_div_functor (C := _C))

181	/-- Existence instance for `HasDivFunctor`. This is the single sorry-carrying
182	site for `divFunctor`. -/
183	noncomputable instance instHasDivFunctor {k : Type u} [Field k]
184	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) : HasDivFunctor C :=
185	  ⟨sorry⟩

187	end PicScheme

189	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
190	
191	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
192	`C/k` **with a `k`-rational point** is the `k`-scheme representing the
193	relative Picard functor `picSharp C` (which, under the rational point, agrees
194	with the étale-sheafified functor of Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main` by Kleiman §2
195	Thm 2.5). The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a
196	disjoint union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the
197	abelian-group-scheme structure is `groupSchemeStructure` below.
198	
199	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
200	
201	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
202	the relative Picard functor `picSharp C`. The single sorry-carrying site for
203	`PicScheme` is the `⟨sorry⟩` instance below, **conditional on
204	`[HasRationalPoint C]`** — without a section the plain relative functor is
205	not representable in general, so an unconditional instance would assert a
206	false statement. Consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
207	hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/
208	class HasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
209	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
210	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
211	  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
212	    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)
213	
214	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
215	integral curve `C/k`, encoded as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.
216	
217	Extracted (`Classical.choose`) from the `HasPicScheme` existence field, so it
218	carries the substantive identity "this scheme represents the relative Picard
219	functor `picSharp C`" through `representable` below — which, after the
220	run-0008 rewire, is a genuine natural bijection onto
221	`Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)`, not an opaque placeholder. -/
222	noncomputable def PicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
223	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
224	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
225	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
226	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
227	  (HasPicScheme.has_pic_scheme (C := C)).choose
228	
229	/-- Existence instance for `HasPicScheme` — **the single genuine FGA sorry of
230	this file**. Mathematical content: Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main` + Cor
231	`cor:algsch` (the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor of a smooth proper
232	geometrically integral curve is representable by a separated `k`-scheme
233	locally of finite type), combined with Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5 (under the
234	`k`-rational point of `[HasRationalPoint C]`, the plain relative functor
235	`picSharp C` coincides with the étale-sheafified one, so the same scheme
236	represents it).
237	
238	The conditionality on `[HasRationalPoint C]` is essential for truth: without
239	a section, `Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` need not even be a Zariski sheaf. -/
240	noncomputable instance instHasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
241	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
242	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
243	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasRationalPoint C] : HasPicScheme C :=
244	  ⟨sorry⟩
245	
246	namespace PicScheme

248	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
249	
250	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
251	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` determines a
252	`T`-point of `Div_{C/k}`, and the dual ideal sheaf
253	`O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` represents a class in `Pic^♯_{C/k}(T)`.
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
256	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
257	
258	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/k}`.
259	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
260	`abelMap`. (The bare `Nonempty` statement is true — the pointwise-zero
261	natural transformation into the group-valued target witnesses it — but the
262	carrier stays opaque until the substantive Abel map arrives with the
263	Quot-scheme engine.) -/
264	class HasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
265	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
266	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
267	  has_abel_map : Nonempty (divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C)
268	
269	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/k}`, sending a relative
270	effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated invertible
271	sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
272	
273	Note the run-0008 rewire changed the TARGET to the real relative Picard
274	functor; the source `divFunctor C` is still an opaque carrier (see
275	`divFunctor`), so this natural transformation remains without defining
276	property until the Quot-scheme engine lands. Extracted from
277	`HasAbelMap.has_abel_map` via `Classical.choice`. -/
278	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
279	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
280	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
281	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasAbelMap C] :
282	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
283	  Classical.choice (HasAbelMap.has_abel_map (C := C))
284	
285	/-- Existence instance for `HasAbelMap`. This is the single sorry-carrying
286	site for `abelMap`. -/
287	noncomputable instance instHasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : HasAbelMap C :=
291	  ⟨sorry⟩

293	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
294	
295	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`: given a
296	surjection `α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable,
297	`Z` representable **by a quasi-projective scheme**, and the first projection
298	`R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper, the quotient `P` is representable.
299	
300	**Run-0008 restructure (truth audit).** The former global instance
301	`instHasSmoothProperQuotient : ∀ α, HasSmoothProperQuotient α := ⟨sorry⟩`
302	asserted that EVERY presheaf receiving any natural transformation is
303	representable — flagrantly false — and was deleted. Moreover the theorem's
304	own hypothesis list is strictly weaker than Kleiman §4 Lem `lm:qt`: it lacks
305	**quasi-projectivity of `Y`** (not currently expressible — Mathlib has no
306	`QuasiProjective` vocabulary), and without it the statement is false: a
307	Hironaka-type free `ℤ/2`-action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold
308	gives a smooth proper equivalence relation satisfying hypotheses (1)–(4)
309	whose quotient étale sheaf is an algebraic space that is not a scheme.
310	
311	`HasSmoothProperQuotient α` therefore survives only as an **explicit
312	use-site hypothesis**: the intended consumer (the FGA assembly, Step 4 of
313	`th:main`) instantiates it for the Abel-map slice `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0`, where `Z`
314	is a quasi-projective open of `Div_{C/k}` and Altman–Kleiman descent
315	genuinely applies. Supplying that instance is part of the `instHasPicScheme`
316	obligation, not a standalone global fact.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`). -/
319

1381	namespace Scheme

1383	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1384	
1385	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1386	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1387	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1388	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1389	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1390	
1391	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1392	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1393	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1394	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1395	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1396	
1397	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1398	
1399	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1400	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1401	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1402	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1403	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1404	morphism of `f`).
1405	
1406	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1407	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1408	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1409	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1410	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1411	
1412	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1413	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1414	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1415	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1416	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1417	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1418	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1419	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1420	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1421	
1422	end Scheme
1423

1425	
1426	The geometric generic-flatness theorem `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
1427	(blueprint `thm:generic_flatness`) is stated and **proved** in the separate
1428	file `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`, as the geometric
1429	glue over the algebraic engine of this file: it consumes the qcqs

1431	(Stacks 01P0/01PC/01I8), which this file deliberately does not import so
1432	that the instance environment of the dévissage proofs above stays fixed. -/
1433	
1434	/-! ## §3–§6. Existence, universality, curve specialisation — moved
1435	
1436	The geometric sections (Nitsure §4 sub-lemmas `flatLocusStratification` and
1437	`flatLocusReduction`, the assembly `flatLocusAssembly`, the main theorem
1438	`flatteningStratification`, its universal property, and the curve

1444	— they pin fully qualified declaration names, which are preserved. -/
